title: Pembina Pipeline Stock Is Down, but Here's Why One Institutional Buyer Invested $316 Million
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Jarislowsky Fraser invested $316 million in Pembina Pipeline during Q3, adding 8.4 million shares despite the stock being down 10% over the past year. The investment suggests long-term potential in North American energy infrastructure with a focus on stable, dividend-generating assets.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock is down 10% but has stable cash generation, a 5.3% dividend yield, and diversified infrastructure assets suggesting potential for gradual recovery
Jarislowsky Fraser reduced its position in the company during Q3
Jarislowsky Fraser reduced its position in the company during Q3
Listed as a top holding in Jarislowsky Fraser's portfolio

title: Want Over $2,100 in Annual Dividends? Invest $12,000 Into Each of These 3 High-Yielding Stocks
author: David Jagielski
description: The article discusses three high-yielding dividend stocks that could generate approximately $2,100 in annual dividends when investing $12,000 in each: Verizon, Bristol Myers Squibb, and Pembina Pipeline, highlighting their dividend yields, financial stability, and potential for steady income.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: 6.3% dividend yield, manageable payout ratio, stable earnings, and modest stock price growth make it a reliable dividend stock

title: Got $5,000? These 3 High-Yielding Dividend Stocks Are Trading Near Their 52-Week Lows
author: David Jagielski
description: Three dividend stocks trading near 52-week lows offer potential investment opportunities: Lockheed Martin, Colgate-Palmolive, and Pembina Pipeline. Each stock provides unique value with stable dividend yields and potential for future growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite a tough quarter with 80% profit decline, the company has strong military and aerospace sector positioning, anticipated free cash flow, and a safe 3.1% dividend yield
Stable consumer staples business, Dividend King status, low payout ratio, modest sales growth, and consistent dividend payments
High 5.6% dividend yield, strong revenue growth, stable energy transportation business with contract-based model, and recent dividend increase

title: Alberta Energy Regulator strengthens Board of Directors with the addition of three new directors
author: N/A
description: The Alberta Energy Regulator has appointed three new members to its Board of Directors and reappointed one current member for a five-year term. The new members bring diverse experience in the energy sector, finance, and Indigenous engagement.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article mentions that the Alberta Energy Regulator has issued an administrative penalty to Pembina Pipeline Corporation for contravening the Public Lands Act, indicating a negative sentiment towards the company's actions.

title: Kayne Anderson Energy Infrastructure Fund Provides Unaudited Balance Sheet Information and Announces Its Net Asset Value and Asset Coverage Ratios at October 31, 2024
author: N/A
description: The Kayne Anderson Energy Infrastructure Fund reported its unaudited balance sheet, net asset value, and asset coverage ratios as of October 31, 2024. The fund's net assets were $2.2 billion, with a net asset value per share of $12.97. The fund's asset coverage ratios were 675% for senior securities and 483% for total leverage.
